concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: Worklist

abstract.p: In the worklist you find all actions which ADAM detected as relevant for this service visit.

entry.p: Section
Description
Reasons/issues
These are the reasons for the service visit that you filled in on the Collect screen. This must also include any customer complaints you received on site.
Errors and warnings
A list of relevant errors and warnings that occurred on this print system. ADAM only shows important errors and warnings.
The following information is displayed:

li.p: MRI: shows which component detected the error
SEA: Systematic Error Analyses; based on trend analysis
SET: Single Event Trigger; shown if the error occurred once or more since last visit
RNK : RaNKed by volume; shown when there is insufficient data to use SEA
Description: describes what type of error it is

entry.p: Click an error line to show additional information.
Click "Show error details" to see when and how often this error/warning occurred.
The Relative usage counter is the most relevant counter for this error.
Click the top-right icons to toggle between the graphic view and the list view.
Click "Show TSM error info" to open the TSM document that explains this error/warning.
Modifications
This section shows the modifications that have not yet been implemented, and which are also shown on the worklist. Click an individual modification to see extra information.
There can be a link to the TSM document that explains the implementation of the modification, for example parts that are needed for the maintenance action.
Maintenance
This section shows maintenance actions and product specific notifications that are relevant during this service visit.
The maintenance action will indicate if 80% of the counter value or time is reached. If they are due (100%), they will be colored red.
A bold line indicates a maintenance group. Click the line to see the list of maintenance items that belong to the group.
Click a maintenance line to show additional information about the action.

li.p: There is a link to the TSM document that explains this maintenance action.
Parts are shown that are needed for this maintenance action (if applicable).

p.i: When an item on the worklist shows a scheduled date, this means that the item was scheduled via the maintenance scheduler in PRISMAservice (On Remote Service) of the CPP backoffice. An agreement with the customer has been made to execute the maintenance item on a fixed date.

A user logs in with a specific service role. By default, only items which are intended for the current service role are presented on the worklist. Product groups can choose to assign service activities to different service roles. To see service activities assigned to different roles than that of the current user, use the role selector (S1) in the top right of the screen:
